channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Law Minister Urges Media Caution on Foreign Policy Discussions Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: Law Minister Azam Nazeer Tarar addressed a press conference alongside Information Minister Ataullah Tarar and Minister of State for Interior Tallal Chaudry, emphasising the importance of exercising ca...

item.title: U.S. and Israel Show Differences in Approach to Iran Conflict Amid Political Pressures
item.description: Ten days after jointly initiating attacks on Iran, the United States and Israel are showing signs of strategic divergence. President Donald Trump, facing domestic political pressure and economic conce...

item.title: Europe Becomes the World's Largest Arms Importer According to SIPRI Report
item.description: According to the latest report from the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI), released on March 9, 2026, the global volume of major arms transfers increased by 9.2% between 2021-20...

item.title: Boniface Mwangi Critiques President Ruto over Alleged Misuse of Public Resources
item.description: Nairobi-based activist Boniface Mwangi has openly criticised President William Ruto, claiming that the administration is turning Kenya into a domain benefiting a few allies. Speaking on Capital in the...

item.title: Zac Lomax faces questions at debut rugby press conference
item.description: Zac Lomax, the rugby union player, attended his first press conference recently, where he faced a range of questions from journalists. Although the full details of the discussion are currently unavail...

item.title: I&B Ministry Temporarily Suspends TRP Ratings for News Channels Over Sensational Coverage
item.description: India’s Ministry of Information and Broadcasting has temporarily suspended the Television Rating Points (TRP) system for four weeks following excessive sensationalism by news channels covering the Ira...

item.title: Analysis of the Legality under International Law of Israeli Strikes on Iranian Oil Refineries and Storage Facilities
item.description: Israeli airstrikes over the weekend targeted oil facilities near Tehran, including the Tondgouyan and Shahran oil refineries – both previously hit in 2025 – and two oil storage sites. According to Ira...

item.title: Government Enrolls 9,000 Poor Households in NHIF for Better Health Access
item.description: In Dodoma Region, the Tanzanian government has successfully enrolled over 9,000 poor households into the National Health Insurance Fund (NHIF), covering around 20,000 beneficiaries. The programme is p...

item.title: CATA Calls for Cameras at Nyanga Taxi Rank After Minibus Torching and Ongoing Violence
item.description: The Cape Amalgamated Taxi Association (CATA) has demanded the installation of surveillance cameras at the Nyanga taxi rank in Cape Town following a series of violent incidents, including the torching ...

item.title: Nigeria: Petrol Price Hike, Governor's Defection, and Court Ruling Dominate Headlines
item.description: Nigeria's news cycle is buzzing today with several significant developments. The Dangote Petroleum Refinery has announced yet another hike in petrol prices, raising the gantry price to ₦1,175 per litr...

item.title: Senior Nigerian Army Officer Umar Farouq Killed During Boko Haram Attack in Borno
item.description: Dem tori be say Lieutenant Colonel Umar Farouq, wey be senior officer for Nigerian Army, don die after Boko Haram attack one military base for Borno State. According to sources wey talk to Sahara Repo...

item.title: KCAA Confirms Petrol Station Near JKIA Complies with Aviation Safety Regulations
item.description: The Kenya Civil Aviation Authority (KCAA) has confirmed that a petrol station being built along Mombasa Road, near Jomo Kenyatta International Airport (JKIA), has passed all aviation safety reviews an...

item.title: Tanzania Security Firms Urged to Employ Only Trained Guards and Pay Salaries on Time
item.description: Dodoma — The Tanzania Security Industry Association (TSIA) in Dodoma Region has advised all private security companies to avoid hiring untrained guards just to save on salaries. Wallace Lussingu, the ...

item.title: Residents evacuated as fire breaks out at Mission seniors’ home
item.description: On Monday evening, a significant fire erupted at the Chartwell Carrington House, a seniors’ residence located on the south side of 7th Avenue in Mission. The flames appeared to originate from the thir...

item.title: Pakistan Starts Second Phase of 5G Spectrum Auction with Major Telecoms Competing
item.description: Pakistan has entered the second round of its 5G spectrum auction in Islamabad, with the three leading telecom companies — Zong, Jazz, and Ufone — bidding aggressively for the crucial 2600 MHz frequenc...

item.title: Government Panel to Address LPG Supply Issues Affecting Hotels and Restaurants
item.description: The Oil Ministry of India has set up a committee to address the sudden shortage of commercial LPG cylinders which is currently affecting the hospitality sector. With rising geopolitical tensions in th...

item.title: Practical tips to reduce fuel use amid rising petrol prices
item.description: Fuel prices across Australia have surged past $2 per litre in several cities, including Darwin, Brisbane and Sydney, as tensions in the Middle East create uncertainty in global energy markets. The Aus...

item.title: Critics Condemn Trump's Threatening 'Death, Fire and Fury' Post Against Iran Amid Ongoing Conflict
item.description: On March 10, 2026, President Donald Trump posted a strongly worded message on Truth Social amid the second week of the U.S.-Israeli military campaign against Iran. He warned that any Iranian attempt t...

item.title: Kim Yo Jong Condemns US-South Korea Military Exercises, Threatens Strengthening of North Korea’s Defence
item.description: Kim Yo Jong, the sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un, has issued a stern warning to the United States and South Korea regarding their ongoing joint military exercises. These annual drills, known...

item.title: Plastic Pollution Reaches Remote Marion Island Despite Local Waste Reductions
item.description: A 30-year study conducted on Marion Island, one of the most isolated islands in the Southern Ocean, reveals a worrying increase in plastic pollution arriving from offshore sources. While local waste f...
